Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. LLM Architecture Gallery 507 upvotes, 39 comments
  2. Reviewing Large Changes with Jujutsu 65 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Why Are Viral Capsids Icosahedral? 49 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Comparing Python Type Checkers: Typing Spec Conformance 32 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 134 upvotes, 26 comments
  6. Bringing Semiconductors to Kazakhstan 19 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 145 upvotes, 31 comments
  8. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance 885 upvotes, 265 comments
  9. MoD sources warn Palantir role at heart of government is threat to UK security 234 upvotes, 64 comments
  10. I Love FreeBSD 92 upvotes, 24 comments
  11. Six ingenious ways how Canon DSLRs used to illuminate their autofocus points 85 upvotes, 24 comments
  12. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 536 upvotes, 211 comments
  13. Stop Sloppypasta 519 upvotes, 210 comments
  14. What every computer scientist should know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 102 upvotes, 34 comments
  15. The 49MB web page 710 upvotes, 308 comments
  16. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 336 upvotes, 139 comments
  17. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant 73 upvotes, 24 comments
  18. Home Assistant waters my plants 144 upvotes, 55 comments
  19. 'Pokémon Go' players unknowingly trained delivery robots with 30B images 53 upvotes, 18 comments
  20. Lazycut: A simple terminal video trimmer using FFmpeg 39 upvotes, 12 comments
  21. Even Faster Asin() Was Staring Right at Me 42 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 642 upvotes, 383 comments
  23. Autoresearch Hub 46 upvotes, 19 comments
  24. LLMs can be exhausting 294 upvotes, 191 comments
  25. Obsession with growth is destroying nature, 150 countries warn 112 upvotes, 67 comments
  26. How I write software with LLMs 368 upvotes, 330 comments
  27. Show HN: Hackerbrief – Top posts on Hacker News summarized daily 35 upvotes, 25 comments
  28. Simple, fast and open-source PHP framework frozen in C-extension 11 upvotes, 9 comments
  29. MCP Server Is Eating Your Context Window. There's a Simpler Way 3 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Nango (YC W23, API Access for Agents and Apps) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 11:30pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
